front: Movement: realist art movement depicting gritty urban life in New York, closely related to 'The Eight'
Movement: name originated from a caption in the Philadelphia Record, disparaged by cartoonist Art Young
Movement: Macbeth Galleries housed the biggest exhibition of this group's artists (1908)
Movement: 'The Eight' exhibition organized by Charcoal Club members to protest the National Academy of Design
Movement: Jacob Riis's How the Other Half Lives parallels this movement's depictions of urban poverty
Movement: connected to the 1913 Armory Show, staged by Arthur B. Davies, Walter Pach, and Walt Kuhn
Movement: leader wrote The Art Spirit, a collection of lectures encouraging interest in color and light as 'means of expression'
Painting: carriage rolling down a snowy street surrounded by brownstone apartment buildings
Painting: depicts a white boxer and the black fighter Joe Gans; title satirizes private sport club membership
back: Both Members of This Club (George Bellows)

front: Painting: referee pushed aside as fighters grapple; borrowed from Belvedere torso; 'stags' are non-member spectators
Painting: one fighter's legs form equilateral triangle, other tangled in ropes; man in background holds bell
Painting: 1913 work showing laundry/clotheslines between tenement buildings, crowd in Lower East Side; trolley to Vesey Street; used H.G. Maratta's color system based on musical notes
Painting: naked boys jumping off dock into East River; critic said they looked 'more like maggots than humans'
Painting: German soldiers hiding behind naked Belgian civilians; artist said Da Vinci 'didn't have a ticket to paint the Last Supper'
Painting: portrait of buck-toothed, bare-chested Irish boy with tattered clothes
Painting: wife in cobalt blue gown, brightly illuminated, right hand resting on keys of dark instrument
Painting: depicts a crowd watching a stylist color a woman's hair through a storefront window
Painting: Ashcan School artist's depiction of a neighborhood bar where he was a regular; paired with Wake of the Ferry
Movement: this artist's 1905 painting The Wrestlers was created to shock the Pennsylvania Academy of Fine Arts; also painted Sulky Boy
Movement: his gallery rejection prompted the movement's leader to form a new group
Movement: Post-Impressionist Maurice Prendergast exhibited with this group but his style differed from the other members
Movement: Stuart Davis studied under this school's leader before gravitating toward cubism
Artist: seascapes inspired by Winslow Homer at Monhegan and Matinicus Islands; The Big Dory shows angular men moving a red and white boat
Artist: Archibald Motley partly inspired by studies under this Ashcan School painter
